Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Raymond" or "spot check" or "QC" or "quality" or "condition clearing" (or similar) using search_emails to find Raymond Chen's QC findings email to Grace Yamamoto, identifying three files where conditions were cleared without supporting documentation.
OE 2: Search Slack messages with a query related to "QC" or "Tyler" or "condition" or "cleared" or "Raymond" or "spot check" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find messages about the QC spot check findings, including Raymond's note about 3 Tyler-cleared files with missing support docs.
OE 3: Search the LOS with a query related to "LN-2026-00603" or the loan's borrower name (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to retrieve the loan record for the first file identified in Raymond's QC findings. Note: Raymond's email describes this file as "already closed" for borrower "Heather Sullivan," but the LOS shows status=processing with borrower Janet Torres (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f). This cross-system discrepancy is an intentional universe feature that tests whether the agent reconciles conflicting data sources.
OE 4: Search the LOS with a query related to "LN-2026-00211" or the loan's borrower name (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to retrieve the loan record for one of the files identified in Raymond's QC findings.
OE 5: Search the LOS with a query related to "LN-2026-00224" or the loan's borrower name (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to retrieve the loan record for the other file identified in Raymond's QC findings.
OE 6: Add an activity note to loan LN-2026-00603 (or its loan_id equivalent) using mortgage_los_add_activity to document the QC finding that conditions were cleared without supporting documentation.
OE 7: Add an activity note to loan LN-2026-00211 (or its loan_id equivalent) using mortgage_los_add_activity to document the QC finding on this file.
OE 8: Add an activity note to loan LN-2026-00224 (or its loan_id equivalent) using mortgage_los_add_activity to document the QC finding on this file.
OE 9: Search emails with a query related to "Destiny Pham" or "redisclosure" or "LN-2026-00613" or "disclosure" or "term change" (or similar) using search_emails to find the TRID redisclosure issue on Destiny Pham's loan where the 30-to-15-year term change required a revised Loan Estimate.
OE 10: Search the LOS with a query related to "LN-2026-00613" or "Destiny Pham" (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan to retrieve the loan details for the file with the redisclosure/TRID issue, including the assigned loan officer (Priya Desai, los_staff_930d770d3309). Note: the emails reference this as "Destiny Pham's file" but the LOS shows the borrower on LN-2026-00613 as Sandra Kang (los_borrower_aa68c38766dc). Destiny Pham has 7 other loans in the LOS (none with number 00613). The agent must follow the loan number from the email subject line ("LN-2026-00613 redisclosure timing") to find the correct record.
OE 11: Send an email with sender elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com using send_email to the loan officer assigned to Destiny Pham's file (Priya Desai at priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com or Carlos Rivera at carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com), cc'ing Sofia Reyes, with details about the TRID disclosure issue and the closing hold.
OE 12: Send a Slack message to #loan-processing (C002) using conversations_add_message summarizing the QC findings on the affected files and the Destiny Pham disclosure hold status, without naming who cleared the conditions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✗✓ 33%

Specifically: tax_return_prior (los_doc_5ed4e78b3f81) shows status approved in LOS with no received_date...' It references the QC finding and quotes the condition description text 'tax_return_prior' from the LOS.
✗Run #2Call 118 references QC finding and missing documentation, but does NOT include a specific los_cond_ condition ID or quote a condition description from the LOS. The agent added new conditions via mortgage_los_add_condition rather than referencing existing condition IDs in the activity note.

Neither UUID condition ID nor any condition description text from the LOS appears in the activity note's detail text.
✓Run #6The activity note at index 268 has action 'QC Review Finding' and detail referencing 'tax_return_prior' as approved without documentation, which quotes the condition description. A follow-up compliance_alert at index 274 also references the doc id los_doc_5ed4e78b3f81 and states QC finding explicitly.

7 An email was sent (via `send_email`) with the sender "elena.merchetti@keystonemortgage.com" to "priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com", the loan officer assigned to LN-2026-00613 in the LOS.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The send_email call confirms sender 'elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com' and primary recipient 'priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com', satisfying both conditions.
✓Run #2Call 120 shows send_email with sender elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com and recipients: ['priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com']. The email was sent to the correct LO after looking up LN-2026-00613 via mortgage_los_get_loan (Call 101).
✗Run #3The email was sent to carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, not priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com. The rubric requires the email to go to the LOS-assigned loan officer (Priya Desai) on LN-2026-00613. The agent found LN-2026-00605 instead of LN-2026-00613.
✓Run #4TC 142 confirms send_email with sender elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com and recipient priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a `send_email` call with sender 'elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com'], which succeeded and returned email_id 'cd38ad04cd444a98b471a22fc2837739'.
✗Run #6The send_email call (index 286) has 'to' set to carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, not priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com. The agent emailed the wrong loan officer — it did not look up LN-2026-00613 to find Priya Desai as the assigned LO.
8 The email sent to the loan officer on the Destiny Pham file includes `sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com` in the CC field.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The send_email call confirms CC field includes 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com'.

No document folder exists at all in the filesystem,' parroting Raymond's email rather than reporting the LOS status as 'processing.' This contradicts Raymond's email claim that the file 'already closed' and correctly reflects the LOS system of record. The LOS data confirmed in tool_call_result_19 shows status='processing' for this loan.
✗Run #6The agent's final response for LN-2026-00603 says it was a QC-flagged loan and notes 'tax_return_prior reopened,' but does not report the LOS status as 'processing' nor identify any discrepancy with Raymond's claim that the file is 'already closed.' The response does name the borrower as 'Janet Torres' (consistent with LOS), but does not surface the closed/processing discrepancy.

✗Run #5The trajectory contains no `mortgage_los_update_condition` calls at all. The agent used `mortgage_los_add_condition` to add new conditions rather than updating existing ones. Since `mortgage_los_get_conditions` returned 0 results for all three loans, the agent chose to create new conditions instead of updating existing ones. The rubric specifically requires `mortgage_los_update_condition`, which was never called.
✗Run #6There are zero mortgage_los_update_condition calls in the entire trajectory. The agent logged activity notes mentioning conditions were 'reopened to required' but never actually called the update condition tool.
